task_completion.visual_evidence: The Docker Desktop application window is visible on the screen.
The 'Containers' section is highlighted or active in the left sidebar of Docker Desktop, showing the list of containers.

task_completion.description: The target container 'test-web-server' is located and initially running.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A container named 'test-web-server' is visible in the list with a 'Running' status or a green indicator.

task_completion.description: The agent clicks the Stop button for the target container.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The mouse cursor is hovering over or clicking the 'Stop' button (typically a square icon) in the row for 'test-web-server'.

task_completion.description: The target container's status changes to stopped/exited.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The status of 'test-web-server' shows 'Exited' and the action button changes from a Stop (square) to a Start (play/triangle) icon.

task_completion.description: The target container remains in the list after being stopped.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'test-web-server' container is still visible in the Docker Desktop container list after its status has changed to Exited, confirming it was not deleted or removed.

integrity.description: The agent used the Docker Desktop GUI to stop the container, not a terminal.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no terminal or command prompt window visible where commands like 'docker stop test-web-server' were typed.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the genuine Docker Desktop application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show the standard Docker Desktop interface, not a text editor, web browser showing a tutorial, or mock interface.

integrity.description: The agent did not use commands or GUI actions to delete the container.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'Delete' (trash can) icon was not clicked, and no 'docker rm' commands are visible in any terminal.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target container is named 'test-web-server' (running the nginx:alpine image). The task requires stopping it via the GUI without removing or deleting it from the container list.
